description: Use this API to modify existing zero sequence mutual impedance data in the working case or to add a new zero sequence mutual coupling to the working case (see PSSE Program Operation Manual, Zero Sequence Mutual Impedance Data). This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr = seq_mutual_data(ibus, jbus, ckt1, kbus, lbus, ckt2, realar)
python_syntax: ierr = seq_mutual_data(ibus, jbus, ckt1, kbus, lbus, ckt2, realar)

parameters.name: ibus
parameters.description: Bus number of the from bus of the first branch. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: jbus
parameters.description: Bus number of the to bus of the first branch. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: ckt1
parameters.description: First branch circuit identifier. (input; ‘1’ by default).

parameters.name: kbus
parameters.description: Bus number of the from bus of the second branch. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: lbus
parameters.description: Bus number of the to bus of the second branch to bus. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: ckt2
parameters.description: Second branch circuit identifier. (input; ‘1’ by default).

realar.description: RM, branch-to-branch mutual resistance (0.0 by default).
XM, branch-to-branch mutual reactance (0.0 by default).
BIJ1, B factor at IBUS end of the first branch (0.0 by default).
BIJ2, B factor at JBUS end of the first branch (1.0 by default).
BKL1, B factor at KBUS end of the second branch (0.0 by default).
BKL2, B factor at LBUS end of the second branch (1.0 by default).

error_codes.description: circuit identifier is more than two characters.
circuit identifier may not start with &
mutual couples a branch to itself.
branch is a zero impedance line.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
data error, warning and information messages; one or more of: - Invalid B factor (<0.0 or >1.0). - B1 is not less than B2.
